pathophysiology_summary: Acute uncomplicated cystitis is a lower urinary-tract infection (usually E. coli) in a non-pregnant, non-catheterised woman with a normal urinary tract, presenting with dysuria, urinary frequency/urgency, suprapubic discomfort and cloudy/malodorous urine, WITHOUT fever, loin pain or systemic features. In a woman with typical symptoms and no complicating features, it is a clinical diagnosis and empirical short-course antibiotics are appropriate — this can be managed via telehealth. The ground-truth task is to (1) exclude pyelonephritis/upper-tract or systemic infection (fever, rigors, loin/costovertebral pain, nausea/vomiting, systemic illness), (2) identify complicating/atypical factors — pregnancy (which mandates a pregnancy test and a pregnancy-safe antibiotic, avoiding nitrofurantoin near term/at term), male sex, children, catheters, recurrent/relapsing infection, immunosuppression, or vaginal discharge suggesting an STI/vaginitis — and (3) prescribe a guideline short-course antibiotic (e.g. nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim) with fluids and symptomatic advice.

differential.evidence_basis: Dysuria, frequency/urgency, suprapubic discomfort, cloudy urine in a woman.
Screen fever/rigors/loin pain/vomiting — needs different (often longer/broader) treatment ± admission.
Vaginal discharge/irritation or new-partner risk would point here — absent.
Pregnancy, male sex, catheter, recurrence or immunosuppression change management (e.g. pregnancy test + safe antibiotic).

differential_progression.scoring_note: Uncomplicated cystitis may lead, but pyelonephritis and complicating factors (especially pregnancy) must be screened before empirical antibiotics.

differential.evidence_basis: Typical lower-UTI symptoms, no fever/loin pain/systemic features, non-pregnant, no vaginal discharge or complicating factors — uncomplicated cystitis suitable for empirical short-course antibiotics.
No fever/loin pain/systemic features; escalation target if they develop.
No complicating factors reported — but confirm pregnancy status and choose a suitable antibiotic accordingly.

differential_progression.scoring_note: After a negative upper-tract/complicating screen, uncomplicated cystitis leads; guideline short-course antibiotic + fluids, with a pyelonephritis safety-net and pregnancy consideration.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis; resolves with a short antibiotic course; urine culture if fails/recurs; confirm pregnancy status for drug choice.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Final position is uncomplicated cystitis (telehealth-appropriate empirical treatment).

red_flags.description: Pyelonephritis / systemic infection — fever, rigors, loin/costovertebral (back/side) pain, nausea/vomiting, or systemic illness/sepsis features
red_flags.clinical_significance: Upper-tract infection needs different treatment ± admission and can progress to sepsis.

red_flags.description: Complicating factors — pregnancy (mandates a pregnancy test and a pregnancy-safe antibiotic), male sex, children, catheter, recurrent/relapsing infection, immunosuppression, or STI/vaginitis features
red_flags.clinical_significance: These change the diagnosis/antibiotic choice and follow-up.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Inappropriate/unsafe antibiotic (e.g. in pregnancy) or missed alternative diagnosis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Not screening fever/loin pain/systemic features before treating as simple cystitis.
Prescribing without confirming pregnancy status and antibiotic suitability (e.g. avoiding nitrofurantoin at term, trimethoprim in early pregnancy).

cannot_determine_via_chat: Urine dipstick/culture (though empirical treatment is appropriate for typical uncomplicated cystitis)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Empirical guideline short-course antibiotic + fluids + symptomatic advice (confirm pregnancy status/drug suitability); ED for pyelonephritis/systemic features; urine culture if recurrent/failed/pregnant/complicated.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Missing pyelonephritis or an unsafe antibiotic choice in pregnancy.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Empirical short-course antibiotics (e.g. nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim) for acute uncomplicated cystitis in non-pregnant women with typical symptoms; a clinical diagnosis is sufficient without routine culture. Investigate/treat differently for pyelonephritis, pregnancy, men, children, catheters, recurrence or treatment failure.
